title: Rabies Rejection Redemption
summary:
After a morning of googling, asking AI, and more consulting with more doctor and nurse friends over a continental breakfast, we decided that I should go back to the ER and tell them that I was getting the rabies shot whether they thought I should or not. As a very conflict-averse person, it was hard for me to put my foot down and advocate for myself, but at the end of the day I received the vaccinations. After receiving three immunoglobulin injections and another shot for the rabies vaccine, I learned that I would need subsequent vaccinations in 3, 7 and 14 days (oh boy)! Another complicating factor is that being a minor procrastinator, I hadn't yet set up new insurance after losing coverage from my job. This meant I needed to use COBRA, which gives you retroactive continuation of your previous insurance if you foot the (expensive) cost for the premium. While definitely not ideal, it beats paying the $19,000 bill that I received from the ER without insurance.

After the stressful previous 24 hours, we were happy to get on the road again head to a climbing area for a few days.

-Colin
